hypothesis:
Given that the workflow successfully identified 31 stable metal superhydrides by screening metal lattices with strong template effects, and that 19 of these exhibit Tc > 100 K primarily in La, Ce, and Th systems, a novel hypothesis extending this work would be:

**Hypothesis:** Ternary metal superhydrides formed by co-doping alkaline earth and rare earth metals into the same lattice framework will exhibit higher superconducting transition temperatures (Tc > 200 K) than their binary counterparts at equivalent pressures, due to synergistic enhancement of both electron-phonon coupling strength and three-dimensional hydrogen network connectivity arising from differential metal-hydrogen template interactions.
